mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, which compresses aggregate corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive returns occur in over 60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with directional accuracy dropping below 50% or Rank IC falling below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 210 and the federal funds rate remains above 4.0 percent, real borrowing costs turn negative and monetary policy tightens, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 58% of observations when both thresholds are breached, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) stays below 0.03 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and rising household income, which expands consumer spending capacity and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or directional accuracy falls below 51% over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sub-1.0 percent quarterly GDP growth and declining retail sales confirms a synchronized slowdown in both aggregate output and consumer demand, which reduces earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% and RSAFS declines
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of weak GDP growth and falling retail sales fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or positive returns dominate in over 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate robust supply-side momentum and construction sector activity, which boosts near-term economic confidence and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO and HOUST values fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy dropping below 52% or Rank IC remaining below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.
